title: Assert statements used for type checking - will be stripped in optimized mode
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 376, 430

The XML expansion functions use assert for type validation:
```python
def _expand_pli_to_xml(...):
    assert isinstance(cot.payload, PliPayload)  # Line 376
    
def _expand_chat_to_xml(...):
    assert isinstance(cot.payload, ChatPayload)  # Line 430
```

When Python runs with -O (optimize) or PYTHONOPTIMIZE=1, all assert statements are removed. This means:
1. Type check is skipped entirely
2. Subsequent code will fail with confusing AttributeError
3. Security-relevant validation is bypassed

Fix: Replace assert with explicit checks that raise appropriate exceptions:
```python
def _expand_pli_to_xml(...):
    if not isinstance(cot.payload, PliPayload):
        raise TypeError(f'Expected PliPayload, got {type(cot.payload).__name__}')
    pli = cot.payload
```

Note: This is especially important for gateway code that processes external input.
